summary: Removed all sign-in/auth UI from citation.is (fully public read-only site) and built the missing public pages and proxy routes.
key_decision: citation.is is a fully public read-only site — no user sign-in is needed anywhere on the frontend. The magic-link auth system built in Phase 104 was removed. Backend admin access (ttruthdesk.claims) remains restricted to owner and tight team only.

removed: openSignInDialog import from DashboardLayout.tsx (replaced with 'Admin access restricted' message)
kept_dormant: server/magicLink.ts — backend endpoints remain but are not linked from UI
magic_link_tokens DB table — kept for potential future admin use

new_pages.description: Audit report detail — shows all verified claims from a document, verdict distribution bar chart, links to ttruthdesk.claims audit page
Knowledge graph entity page — shows all claims mentioning the entity, entity type icon, support/refuted counts, links to knowledge graph
Developer documentation — full REST API docs with collapsible endpoints, code examples in curl/Python/JS, MCP integration guide, OpenAPI spec link, LLM grounding instructions

homepage_improvements: Live recently verified claims from /api/external/public/claims (replaces hardcoded SAMPLE_CLAIMS)
Live total claim count from public registry API (shown in hero text and stats grid)
Recent claims are clickable links to /claims/:id
nav_changes: Added Developers link (Code2 icon) to Nav.tsx
Removed Sign In button (was never present in Nav — was in DashboardLayout only)
constraints_respected: No user sign-in on public site
All proxy routes server-side (no CORS issues)
next_phase_suggestions: Phase 106: Improve /audit/:id to use a dedicated document-level API endpoint if ttruthdesk.claims exposes one (currently fetches first 200 claims and filters client-side)
Phase 107: Add JSON-LD structured data to /developers page for SEO
Phase 108: Add sitemap.xml and robots.txt updates for new routes
Phase 109: Add search.json / sitemap for knowledge graph entities
Phase 110: Build admin-only dashboard on ttruthdesk.claims (separate from citation.is)
